name: Laguna del Prado — circular by Pozuelo de Calatrava
name_original: Laguna del Prado — circular por Pozuelo de Calatrava
region_intro: Jogging around Pozuelo De Calatrava offers diverse routes through a landscape shaped by its unique volcanic heritage and tranquil natural spaces. The region, part of the Campo de Calatrava, features varied topography suitable for physical activity. Key natural features include the Laguna del Prado, a saline lagoon and wildlife refuge, which provides a dedicated running loop. This geological setting offers a blend of natural beauty and varied terrain for runners. Best jogging routes around Pozuelo De Calatrava The most popular jogging route is Laguna del Prado — circular by Pozuelo de Calatrava, a 2.5 miles (4.0 km) trail that takes 28 minutes to complete. This easy route circles the Laguna del Prado, known for its birdlife and primarily paved surfaces. Another top favourite among local runners is Miguelturra loop from Miguelturra, a moderate 3.1 miles (5.0 km) path. This route explores the natural surroundings of the Campo de Calatrava region, offering a glimpse into its tranquil landscapes. Local runners also love the Running loop from Miguelturra, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail leading through the natural spaces of the area, often completed in about 1 hour 5 minutes. Jogging around Pozuelo De Calatrava is defined by its volcanic landscape, natural spaces, and the serene Laguna del Prado. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Pozuelo De Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore Pozuelo De Calatrava's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Pozuelo de Calatrava?
A: There are over 50 running routes available around Pozuelo de Calatrava, catering to various fitness levels and preferences. These routes explore the region's unique volcanic landscape and tranquil natural spaces.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, there are several easy running routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211472556/laguna-del-prado-circular-by-pozuelo-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Prado — circular by Pozuelo de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt;. This 4 km (2.5 miles) trail is primarily on paved surfaces and offers a pleasant experience around the Laguna del Prado, known for its birdlife.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Pozuelo de Calatrava?
A: The running trails in Pozuelo de Calatrava offer varied terrain, influenced by the region's volcanic heritage. You'll find routes with gentle gradients and paved surfaces, especially around natural reserves like Laguna del Prado, as well as more challenging paths through the distinctive geological landscape of the Campo de Calatrava.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Pozuelo de Calatrava are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211472556/laguna-del-prado-circular-by-pozuelo-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Prado — circular by Pozuelo de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering a scenic loop around the natural reserve.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Pozuelo de Calatrava?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3710191" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Prado&lt;/a&gt;, a significant saline lagoon and wildlife refuge known for its diverse aquatic birdlife. The broader Campo de Calatrava also features a unique volcanic landscape with maars and interesting geological formations. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2659589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Isidro Hermitage and Maares Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views.

Q: Are there any running routes that are particularly good for families?
A: For families, the easier, flatter routes are ideal. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1211472556/laguna-del-prado-circular-by-pozuelo-de-calatrava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Prado — circular by Pozuelo de Calatrava&lt;/a&gt; is a great option due to its manageable distance (4 km) and mostly paved surfaces, making it accessible for various ages and fitness levels.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails around Pozuelo de Calatrava?
A: Many natural trails and open spaces in the Campo de Calatrava region are suitable for running with dogs, provided they are kept under control. It's always advisable to check local regulations for specific natural reserves or protected areas like Laguna del Prado to ensure dog access is permitted and to follow any leash requirements.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Pozuelo de Calatrava?
A: The tranquil and hospitable character of the Manchegan villages, coupled with the natural environment, makes Pozuelo de Calatrava appealing for outdoor activities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for running, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential chill of winter.

Q: Are there any longer running routes for more experienced runners?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38155460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Miguelturra&lt;/a&gt; offer a substantial distance of 10.4 km (6.5 miles). This route takes you through the natural spaces of the area, providing a good workout for experienced runners.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Pozuelo de Calatrava?
A: The running routes in Pozuelo de Calatrava are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Runners often praise the serene landscapes, the unique volcanic scenery, and the opportunity to observe birdlife, especially around the Laguna del Prado. The variety of routes also caters to different preferences.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer viewpoints?
A: Yes, the volcanic landscape of the Campo de Calatrava region provides opportunities for scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2659589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Isidro Hermitage and Maares Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is a notable spot that offers expansive views of the distinctive geological formations, which can be incorporated into longer running excursions.
tips:
The Prado Lagoon is a saline volcanic lagoon with seasonal waters, located in the municipality of Pozuelo de Calatrava. Hydrographically it belongs to the Guadiana river basin.

Its origin is pseudokarstic, since it was caused by a phreatomagmatic explosion at a fracture junction located in a sedimentary basin. This has favored surface ponding like this semi-arid lagoon, with sulphated-magnesium-sodic chloride waters. Due to its salinity, the vegetation is relegated to the margins, mainly salt marshes and halonitrophilous plants. The contribution of rainwater from the town has altered that salinity, allowing the appearance of reeds, castanets and even white poplars.

During periods when it is flooded, you can see black-headed gulls, black-headed gulls, avocets, stilts and plovers, as well as pink flamingos.

Within the Campo de Calatrava region, this municipality attracts the visitor's attention because it is close to two salty lagoons of volcanic origin, Argamasilla and Inesperada; formerly used as medicinal waters. Like other thermal springs where the Infante Don Carlos ordered the construction of the Baños de Fuensanta. In addition to its natural assets, the religious ones are important, in the form of a church and a sanctuary that we must not miss.
Its 17th century church dedicated to Saint John the Baptist was declared a B.I.C. in 1989. Its nave is covered by a barrel vault and has a polygonal apse and two doorways under porticoes, one of them with Tiara Papal. It is one of the few churches that preserves its three original altarpieces from the 18th century, the most important being that of the High Altar made of walnut wood without polychrome and with allegories of the Trinity. In the Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora de los Santos we can also admire a beautiful altarpiece in Churrigueresque forms. The sanctuary is on the outskirts of the town and was built on an old Arab fortress. It has a single nave in which we will see wall paintings and a beautiful Mudejar coffered ceiling.
